title: Future-phase cleanups: ATTACH narrow-except + _cnt_inc hoist + per-phase closeout_drift_resolution rewrite
intent: Resolve the three documented future-phase carry-forwards from T2F + T2G + T2H now (no further deferral per operator). Three deliverables, three commits, three reviewer-chain verification rounds. (A) Replace the bare `except Exception:` at src/engine/cycle_runner.py:81-82 (T2H state, post-import-hoist) with `except sqlite3.OperationalError:` so non-OperationalError surfaces propagate rather than being silently swallowed; tighten the upstream `src/state/db.py::get_trade_connection_with_world` ATTACH at db.py:103 by wrapping it in the same narrow-except idiom (currently bare, raises everything; the asymmetry vs the cycle_runner mirror is itself a defect). Add a relationship test that asserts a non-sqlite3.OperationalError raised inside the ATTACH branch propagates to the caller in BOTH sites. (B) Hoist the 17 function-scope `from src.observability.counters import increment as _cnt_inc` imports across 5 source files (src/state/db.py × 1, src/state/chain_reconciliation.py × 9, src/state/portfolio.py × 2, src/venue/polymarket_v2_adapter.py × 2, src/execution/harvester.py × 3) to module-level. Per-file safety probe: `python -c 'import src.<module>'` after each hoist; if ImportError, REVERT that file's hoist and document the actual circular path in BATCH_DONE. Goal: zero function-scope `_cnt_inc` imports remaining where safe; documented reverts where not safe. (C) Rewrite the 6 T1 phase.json `closeout_drift_resolution` fields (T1F, T1BD, T1C, T1E, T1G, T1H) from the identical boilerplate '`T2F-DOC-DRIFT-CLEANED: typed counter sink landed in T2F B1 (2026-05-05)...`' to phase-specific resolution prose citing the exact LOW each phase carried forward and which T2 phase resolved it.
test_commands: python -c "import src.state.db, src.state.chain_reconciliation, src.state.portfolio, src.venue.polymarket_v2_adapter, src.execution.harvester, src.engine.cycle_runner"

asserted_invariants.text: src/engine/cycle_runner.py post-T2H ATTACH branch (currently `try: ... attached = ...; conn.execute('ATTACH DATABASE ? AS world', ...) except Exception: pass` at lines 77-82) is rewritten to `except sqlite3.OperationalError as exc: logger.warning('ATTACH world failed (non-fatal): %r', exc)` (or equivalent narrow-except + log). NO bare `except Exception:` remains in the get_connection function body. Verification: `git grep -n 'except Exception' src/engine/cycle_runner.py` returns 0 matches inside get_connection (lines 67-90 region); `git grep -n 'except sqlite3.OperationalError' src/engine/cycle_runner.py` returns ≥ 1 match in get_connection's ATTACH branch.

src/state/db.py::get_trade_connection_with_world (lines 99-105 region) ATTACH call at line 103 is wrapped in a `try / except sqlite3.OperationalError as exc: logger.warning(...)` block (currently bare — any OperationalError or other exception during ATTACH currently raises uncaught). The narrow-except mirrors the cycle_runner pattern: OperationalError → log + continue with un-attached connection (caller may detect via PRAGMA database_list); any other exception class still propagates. Verification: `git grep -n 'except sqlite3.OperationalError' src/state/db.py` returns ≥ 1 match inside get_trade_connection_with_world; the function still returns a sqlite3.Connection (never None — degrade-to-None is connect_or_degrade's contract, not this function's).

tests/test_attach_narrow_except.py asserts that when a non-sqlite3.OperationalError exception is raised from inside the ATTACH branch, the exception PROPAGATES to the caller in BOTH sites: (a) src.engine.cycle_runner.get_connection, and (b) src.state.db.get_trade_connection_with_world. AMD-T2I-2: the test is parametrized over the exception class set [RuntimeError, sqlite3.DatabaseError, PermissionError] with one `pytest.raises(class)` per parametrization — the test must NOT pass when caught on a different exception class than declared (i.e., the test is configured so monkeypatched conn.execute raises exactly the parametrized class, and pytest.raises asserts the same class). Conversely, when sqlite3.OperationalError is raised, the function returns normally (cycle_runner: with degrade semantics intact via T2G; db.py: with the un-attached connection — the AMD-T2I-1 swallow-and-detect contract).

AMD-T2I-1: behavior-change relationship test. When sqlite3.OperationalError is raised from inside the ATTACH branch (simulated via monkeypatch of conn.execute on the ATTACH call to raise sqlite3.OperationalError; the prior PRAGMA database_list call must succeed normally), the narrow-except swallows + logs and the function returns a sqlite3.Connection where 'world' is NOT present in `PRAGMA database_list`. tests/test_attach_narrow_except.py::test_attach_oe_swallowed_returns_unattached_conn_caller_can_detect_via_pragma asserts in BOTH sites: (a) src.engine.cycle_runner.get_connection — `conn = get_connection(); names = {row[1] for row in conn.execute('PRAGMA database_list').fetchall()}; assert 'world' not in names`; (b) src.state.db.get_trade_connection_with_world — same assertion shape. The contract is 'callers can detect the un-attached state defensively via PRAGMA database_list before issuing cross-DB queries'; downstream code that assumes 'world' is always attached must update to the detection idiom.

Function-scope `from src.observability.counters import increment as _cnt_inc` imports are hoisted to module-level in each of 5 source files (src/state/db.py, src/state/chain_reconciliation.py, src/state/portfolio.py, src/venue/polymarket_v2_adapter.py, src/execution/harvester.py). Verification: `git grep -c 'from src.observability.counters import increment as _cnt_inc' src/state/db.py src/state/chain_reconciliation.py src/state/portfolio.py src/venue/polymarket_v2_adapter.py src/execution/harvester.py` shows the per-file count dropping from {1, 9, 2, 2, 3} pre-T2I to {1, 1, 1, 1, 1} post-T2I (one module-level import per file replaces all function-scope occurrences). Where a hoist surfaces an actual circular import (verified via `python -c 'import src.<module>'` ImportError), that file's hoist is REVERTED. AMD-T2I-3: a revert is acceptable only if the BATCH_DONE structured return for that file includes ALL THREE of: (a) the exact ImportError traceback observed (verbatim, including the `ImportError: cannot import name ... from partially initialized module ...` or equivalent line + the full traceback chain), (b) the import chain that creates the cycle, expressed in the form `<file_being_hoisted> imports src.observability.counters which (transitively) imports <file_being_hoisted>` — i.e., name the actual A→B→...→A cycle path concretely, not 'something is circular', (c) a one-sentence rationale why the cycle cannot be broken via API change (e.g., 'src.observability.counters depends on src.state.<module> only via TYPE_CHECKING and runtime hoist requires breaking that contract'). Lazy reverts (e.g., 'hoist failed; reverted' without the three fields) are NOT acceptable — verifier rejects the BATCH_DONE and executor must re-investigate. Per-file 'hoist OK' or 'hoist REVERTED — cycle: A → B → A — rationale: <one sentence>' status is enumerated in the executor's structured return. The invariant is satisfied if (a) every hoist that does not surface an ImportError is applied, AND (b) every revert is documented with the three-field concrete evidence contract above.

After the hoist (and any required reverts), `python -c 'import src.state.db, src.state.chain_reconciliation, src.state.portfolio, src.venue.polymarket_v2_adapter, src.execution.harvester, src.engine.cycle_runner'` succeeds with exit code 0; no ImportError, ModuleNotFoundError, or circular-import error. The existing test surfaces (tests/test_observability_counters.py 17 tests, tests/test_counter_caplog_ledger.py 36 tests, tests/test_cycle_runner_db_lock_degrade.py) continue to pass byte-identically (the hoist is structurally pure: no runtime semantics change; same call-site behavior, same emit count).

The 6 T1 phase.json files (T1F, T1BD, T1C, T1E, T1G, T1H) each have a `_planner_notes.closeout_drift_resolution` field that is phase-specific text (NOT identical boilerplate). Required content per phase: T1F cites the files_touched line drift 1395-1397→1456-1458 in db.py merge surface, with note that invariants are byte-identical at the new line numbers per critic verification; T1BD cites that lifecycle_events.py listing was inspection-only (read-only context, never mutated by the executor); T1C cites that wrapper-only placement matches phase.json invariant text verbatim and preserves the G-HARV3 antibody (test_openmeteo_p_raw_lineage_does_not_write_tigge_training_pair); T1E cites that the negative-env validation gap was RESOLVED by T2F-NEGATIVE-ENV-VALIDATION-LOUD-FAIL; T1G cites that the FOK/FAK fixture LOW was deferred per T2F planner_notes.out_of_scope_explicit pending production telemetry showing usage AND must contain the verbatim phrase 'Not a defect; not resolved in T2I either' (AMD-T2I-5: prevents drift toward false resolution claim — T1G is a documented defer, not a resolution); T1H cites that the ImportError fallback drift was RESOLVED by T2F-CENSUS-FILL-AUTHORITY-FAIL-CLOSED. Verification: `python -c "import json; texts = [json.load(open(f))['_planner_notes']['closeout_drift_resolution'] for f in ['docs/operations/task_2026-05-04_zeus_may3_review_remediation/phases/'+p+'/phase.json' for p in ['T1F','T1BD','T1C','T1E','T1G','T1H']]]; assert len(set(texts)) == 6, 'all six fields must be distinct, not boilerplate'; assert all('T2F-DOC-DRIFT-CLEANED' not in t for t in texts), 'old boilerplate must be removed'; t1g_text = json.load(open('docs/operations/task_2026-05-04_zeus_may3_review_remediation/phases/T1G/phase.json'))['_planner_notes']['closeout_drift_resolution']; assert 'Not a defect; not resolved in T2I either' in t1g_text, 'T1G must preserve verbatim defer language per AMD-T2I-5'"`.

deliverable_a_evidence.cycle_runner_attach_block: src/engine/cycle_runner.py:77-83 — `try: attached = {row[1] for row in conn.execute('PRAGMA database_list').fetchall()}; if 'world' not in attached: conn.execute('ATTACH DATABASE ? AS world', (str(ZEUS_WORLD_DB_PATH),)) except Exception: pass`. Bare-except swallow is the T2G C-2 LOW. Post-T2H import hoist is in place (ZEUS_WORLD_DB_PATH at module level), so the only edit T2I needs is the except clause + log line.
deliverable_a_evidence.db_py_attach_block: src/state/db.py:99-105 — `def get_trade_connection_with_world() -> sqlite3.Connection: conn = get_trade_connection(); attached = {row[1] for row in conn.execute('PRAGMA database_list').fetchall()}; if 'world' not in attached: conn.execute('ATTACH DATABASE ? AS world', (str(ZEUS_WORLD_DB_PATH),)) return conn`. Currently NO try/except — any OperationalError raises uncaught. Asymmetry vs cycle_runner: db.py raises everything, cycle_runner swallows everything; both are wrong. T2I unifies both to narrow-except + log.
deliverable_a_evidence.asymmetry_observation: The brief described db.py:99-104 as 'pre-existing bare-except' but fresh grep shows db.py:99-104 has NO try/except at all (the bare except is ONLY in cycle_runner.py:81). Brief used 'pre-existing pattern' loosely; T2I tightens BOTH sites symmetrically (cycle_runner: bare→narrow; db.py: none→narrow with same OperationalError-only swallow + log). This is a planner-side reality-correction surfacing in evidence_grep, not a brief deviation.

deliverable_b_evidence.function_scope_cnt_inc_count: 17 function-scope imports across 5 source files (NOT 6 — brief listed scripts/state_census.py but `grep -rn 'import increment as _cnt_inc' scripts/state_census.py` returns 0 matches; the census uses a different import path or no _cnt_inc alias). Per-file: src/state/db.py:118 (1), src/state/chain_reconciliation.py:535,543,546,553,644,652,655,663,680 (9), src/state/portfolio.py:1176,1708 (2), src/venue/polymarket_v2_adapter.py:321,574 (2), src/execution/harvester.py:551,564,1742 (3).
deliverable_b_evidence.module_level_target_per_file: Each of the 5 files gains 1 module-level `from src.observability.counters import increment as _cnt_inc` (17 function-scope occurrences → 5 module-level + 0 function-scope, ideal). Module-level target line: top of imports block, after stdlib/third-party imports.
deliverable_b_evidence.circular_import_risk_assessment: src.observability.counters does NOT import from src.state.* / src.venue.* / src.execution.* (verified by reading src/observability/counters.py imports — it only imports stdlib threading + logging + typing). Therefore module-level hoist should not surface a circular import. However, the executor MUST verify per-file via `python -c 'import src.<module>'` after each hoist; if ImportError surfaces (e.g., due to indirect import chain through something the file already imports), the hoist for that file is REVERTED.
deliverable_b_evidence.critic_t2f_approval_record: Critic at T2F review explicitly approved function-scope as defensible per T1A precedent (the function-scope inline imports were a deliberate executor choice for late-binding). T2I revisits this only because operator instruction supersedes the deferral; the per-file probe is the safety net to prevent regression.

deliverable_c_evidence.current_boilerplate_text: All 6 T1 phase.json files contain the identical text 'T2F-DOC-DRIFT-CLEANED: typed counter sink landed in T2F B1 (2026-05-05). All T1 phases share src/observability/counters.py. No per-phase DDL or schema drift outstanding.' Located at: T1F:72, T1BD:97, T1C:86, T1E:83, T1G:89, T1H:79.

per_phase_resolution_targets.T1F: Resolution: T1A merge surface line drift 1395-1397 → 1456-1458 in db.py — invariants T1A-DDL-SINGLE-SOURCE + T1A-DB-IMPORTS-SCHEMA byte-identical at new line numbers per critic verification (commit 76ef3796 invariants.jsonl close entry). No invariant text rewrite required; line numbers are not load-bearing across phase closures.
per_phase_resolution_targets.T1BD: Resolution: lifecycle_events.py listed in files_touched but the executor never wrote to it — the file was inspection-only context for the chain-reconciliation 4-field freeze. Critic at T1BD review verified zero git-diff against lifecycle_events.py; the listing is an over-permissive scope artifact. Documenting as 'read-only context, never mutated' rather than removing from files_touched preserves the audit trail of which files the executor consulted.
per_phase_resolution_targets.T1C: Resolution: wrapper-only placement of authority/lineage guards in maybe_write_learning_pair() matches the phase.json invariant text 'wrapper refuses to call harvest_settlement without explicit source/lineage authority' verbatim; the spirit-vs-text concern was about whether the guards belonged INSIDE harvest_settlement's body. Critic verified that wrapper placement preserves the G-HARV3 antibody (tests/test_openmeteo_p_raw_lineage.py::test_openmeteo_p_raw_lineage_does_not_write_tigge_training_pair) which would have been broken by inside-body placement (openmeteo legitimately writes training_allowed=0 audit pairs). Decision-of-record: wrapper-only is the structurally correct placement per Fitz #1 (structural decision, not patch).
per_phase_resolution_targets.T1E: Resolution: negative-env validation gap (C-1 LOW: ZEUS_DB_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS<0 produces negative timeout, loud-fail at connect not at parse-time) was RESOLVED by T2F-NEGATIVE-ENV-VALIDATION-LOUD-FAIL invariant. Post-T2F src/state/db.py:_db_busy_timeout_s raises ValueError at parse time on negative input; tests/test_sqlite_busy_timeout.py::test_negative_env_rejected_at_parse_time asserts. Boilerplate field replaced with this concrete resolution citation.
per_phase_resolution_targets.T1G: Resolution: FOK/FAK structural-argument-not-parametrized-fixture LOW (C-1) was DEFERRED in T2F per planner_notes.out_of_scope_explicit ('FOK/FAK fixture (T1G C-1) — promotion to fixture is deferred to follow-up phase if production telemetry shows usage; out of T2F scope per planner Round-3'). Decision-of-record: deferral is intentional pending production telemetry showing FOK/FAK usage frequency that warrants fixture promotion. Not a defect; not resolved in T2I either.
per_phase_resolution_targets.T1H: Resolution: ImportError fallback drift (C-1 LOW: scripts/state_census.py hardcoded duplicate of FILL_AUTHORITY_VENUE_CONFIRMED_FULL='venue_confirmed_full' on src.state.portfolio import failure) was RESOLVED by T2F-CENSUS-FILL-AUTHORITY-FAIL-CLOSED invariant. Post-T2F the census exits non-zero with 'authority constant unavailable; refusing to classify' rather than silently using the duplicate string; tests/test_state_census.py::test_census_fail_closed_on_missing_authority_constant asserts. Boilerplate field replaced with this concrete resolution citation.

loc_estimate_basis.Deliverable A (cycle_runner.py + db.py +: ~80 LOC — cycle_runner.py:81 except clause rewrite + log line (~5 LOC), db.py:103 wrap in try/except + log line (~8 LOC), tests/test_attach_narrow_except.py NEW (~65 LOC: 4 test cases × ~15 LOC each — cycle_runner OperationalError swallowed, cycle_runner non-OperationalError propagates, db.py OperationalError swallowed, db.py non-OperationalError propagates).
loc_estimate_basis.Deliverable B (5 files × hoist): ~30 LOC if all hoists succeed — each file gains 1 module-level import (+1 LOC) and removes N function-scope imports (-N LOC); net per-file: db.py -1+1=0, chain_reconciliation.py -9+1=-8, portfolio.py -2+1=-1, polymarket_v2_adapter.py -2+1=-1, harvester.py -3+1=-2. Total LOC delta: -12 net (12 lines removed). LOC churn: ~22 lines touched. If any hoist reverts, that file's net delta returns to 0.
loc_estimate_basis.Deliverable C (6 phase.json rewrites): ~80 LOC — each closeout_drift_resolution field grows from ~1 line of boilerplate to ~3-4 lines of phase-specific prose; 6 files × ~5 LOC growth = ~30 LOC + ~50 LOC from text-replacement diff churn.
loc_estimate_basis.total: ~190-320 LOC depending on hoist revert count and phase.json prose verbosity (matches brief guidance ~250-350)

_planner_notes.k_class_classification: MIXED. (A) is K1 STATE TRUTH — db connection acquisition path; not LIVE BOUNDARY because no order submit / no settlement transition; the narrow-except has clear blast radius (a non-OperationalError that previously propagated will continue to propagate; an OperationalError that previously crashed will now log + continue with un-attached connection — observable but bounded). (B) is K2 hygiene — the hoist is structurally pure refactor with byte-identical runtime semantics (every `_cnt_inc(...)` call site continues to call the same function). (C) is K2 docs — phase.json _planner_notes field rewrite. No K0 LIVE BOUNDARY edge in T2I → no security-reviewer per T1 retrospective doctrine.
_planner_notes.reviewer_set_rationale: critic-opus + verifier-sonnet + git-master-sonnet. K1 STATE TRUTH (Deliverable A) is the highest-blast-radius surface in T2I; opus critic adversarially probes the narrow-except logic and the test fidelity. No security-reviewer because no LIVE BOUNDARY (no SDK contact, no venue side effect, no live-money state-truth edge). Persistent reviewer triplet (critic a03538fb0b5f999ed + verifier a62719f768a7c2478 + git-master per s4 telemetry) continues per T2 retrospective recommendation.
_planner_notes.predecessor_dependency: T2F + T2G + T2H landed (commits 7dac7096 + ee94539f + ... per s4 + s5 telemetry). T2I consumes T2F-COUNTER-SINK-TYPED-API + T2F-EVERY-T1-COUNTER-EMITS-VIA-SINK + T2H-CYCLE-RUNNER-WORLD-PATH-MODULE-LEVEL — modifying cycle_runner.py:81 before T2H's import hoist landed would have collided with T2H's import-block edit. Mainline closed at f5654ef0 with all three T2 phases merged (per brief).
deviation_anticipation: Deliverable A: executor may discover that the db.py:103 ATTACH wrap surfaces existing test surfaces that depended on OperationalError propagating uncaught from get_trade_connection_with_world (the function currently raises everything; tightening to swallow OperationalError + log is a behavior change). If existing tests assert on the raised exception, surface as deviation in BATCH_DONE — do not silently rewrite tests. Most likely: tests use connect_or_degrade for lock-aware paths and never call get_trade_connection_with_world inside a context where ATTACH would fail, so no test regression expected. Still: per-file probe required.

Deliverable B: executor may surface concrete import-cycle reverts (per brief: 'some hoists may be impossible'). The most likely revert candidate is src/state/chain_reconciliation.py because it has 9 function-scope occurrences which suggests the original executor (T1BD) deliberately chose function-scope to avoid an import-time effect. If the hoist surfaces a circular import via src.state.* indirect chain, that single-file revert is documented and the per-deliverable invariant T2I-CNT-INC-MODULE-LEVEL-WHERE-SAFE accepts the partial outcome. The per-file safety probe is the canonical adjudicator.

Deliverable C: executor may discover additional drift items not currently documented (e.g., a T1 invariant whose evidence file:line citations rotted post-T2). If any phase's closeout_drift_resolution prose surfaces a new drift not in the planner_notes.deliverable_c_evidence.per_phase_resolution_targets table, surface as deviation — do not silently expand the resolution scope.

Deliverable A asymmetry-correction (db.py currently NO except, brief said 'pre-existing bare-except'): executor follows the planner_notes correction (db.py wraps from scratch with narrow-except + log; cycle_runner converts bare-except to narrow-except + log). The brief's 'pre-existing pattern' phrasing is reality-corrected by planner_notes.deliverable_a_evidence.asymmetry_observation.
_planner_notes.category_impossible_target: Post-T2I: (a) the 'silent ATTACH failure swallow' pattern is gone — both ATTACH sites use narrow-except + log, so non-OperationalError surfaces propagate by construction. (b) The 'function-scope late-binding _cnt_inc import' pattern is reduced to only the documented-revert minimum (where circular-import is provably-real). (c) The 6 T1 phase.json closeout_drift_resolution fields are phase-specific decision-of-record entries, no longer boilerplate that disguises the per-phase resolution provenance.
out_of_scope_explicit: src/engine/cycle_runner.py:847 cargo-cult inline import block: RECLASSIFIED as NOT_A_DEFECT per the 2026-05-05T12:30Z reclassification ledger entry. The 7 inline imports inside the EntriesBlockRegistry try/except Exception block are LOAD-BEARING ANTIBODY pattern explicitly labeled 'REGISTRY-GUARDED SHORT-CIRCUIT (2026-05-04 antibody)' with comment 'Registry must never break the cycle — fail soft, log, continue.' Hoisting any of those imports to module-level would let any sub-module ImportError (riskguard, heartbeat_supervisor, ws_gap_guard, entry_forecast_rollout) crash the live cycle, regressing the explicit fail-soft design. Critic at T2H plan review correctly observed 'non-trivial import-time complexity' but mis-pattern-matched to the T2H lines 73/79 cleanup target. T2I MUST NOT touch lines 842-883 of cycle_runner.py. The reclassification is final; no further phase will revisit this surface absent an explicit operator override.

Adding new test surfaces beyond tests/test_attach_narrow_except.py (Deliverable A's relationship test). Deliverables B and C are structural-purity and docs-only; existing test surfaces are the regression contract. If the executor finds that the existing T2F counter test suite does not actually exercise the post-hoist module-level import shape, the executor may add ONE one-liner assertion inside an existing test file (matching the T2H precedent for the import-shape probe), but adding a new test FILE for B or C is OUT OF SCOPE.
Modifying src/observability/counters.py itself. The hoist target name `increment` and the alias `_cnt_inc` are stable; if the executor wants to rename or change the API, that is a separate phase.

Modifying scripts/state_census.py. Despite the brief listing it in Deliverable B's surface set, fresh grep shows zero function-scope `_cnt_inc` imports in scripts/state_census.py (it uses a different observability pattern or no counter at all). The file is OUT OF SCOPE for Deliverable B; if the executor wants to investigate why census doesn't emit counters via the typed sink, that is a separate phase.
Adding monitoring/alerting infrastructure or modifying docs/runbooks/T2G_database_locked_degrade_runbook.md. T2H landed the runbook; T2I does not touch operator-facing docs.
scope_amendments_anticipated: If the per-file safety probe for Deliverable B reveals that >2 of the 5 files cannot be hoisted (i.e., the function-scope late-binding was deliberate for >40% of the surface), the executor SHOULD pause and surface the pattern as a deviation rather than executing partial hoists. Coordinator decides whether to proceed with partial-hoist outcome or split Deliverable B into a follow-up investigation phase.

If Deliverable A's narrow-except in db.py introduces a test regression in tests/test_*.py that the executor did not anticipate, the executor surfaces it in BATCH_DONE rather than silently updating the test. Coordinator + critic decide whether the test was over-asserting on the raises behavior or whether the narrow-except is itself wrong.
_planner_notes.split_batch_rationale: Three deliverables are landed as three separate commits per the brief ('reviewer chain can verify atomically'). B1 = Deliverable A (narrow-except + new test); B2 = Deliverable B (hoist + per-file probes); B3 = Deliverable C (6 phase.json rewrites). Each commit gets its own critic + verifier verification; git-master closes all three. If any single deliverable surfaces blocking deviations, the others can still proceed independently.

batch_plan.B1: Deliverable A: src/engine/cycle_runner.py narrow-except + src/state/db.py wrap + tests/test_attach_narrow_except.py NEW. ~80 LOC. Single commit. Reviewer chain: critic + verifier. K1 STATE TRUTH surface; highest-blast-radius batch in T2I.
batch_plan.B2: Deliverable B: hoist 17 function-scope `_cnt_inc` imports across 5 files, with per-file safety probe + documented reverts where probe fails. ~30 LOC if all succeed. Single commit. Reviewer chain: critic + verifier. K2 hygiene.
batch_plan.B3: Deliverable C: rewrite 6 T1 phase.json closeout_drift_resolution fields with phase-specific prose. ~80 LOC. Single commit. Reviewer chain: critic + verifier (text-only review; verifier verifies the json-distinct-fields assertion). K2 docs.
